original_question: A grasshopper starts at vertex \( A \) of a regular hexagon with vertices labeled \( A, B, C, D, E, F \) in order. Each second, it jumps to one of the two adjacent vertices with equal probability. It keeps jumping until it has visited all six vertices. What is the probability that the grasshopper is back at vertex \( A \) when this happens?
